challenges.title: SVG Shapes
challenges.description: Create an inline SVG element containing a circle and a rectangle (rect). Give each shape fill colors. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create an inline SVG with a circle and a rectangle -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
lower = html.lower()
assert '<svg' in lower, 'Should contain an <svg> element'
assert '</svg>' in lower, 'Should close the <svg> element'
assert '<circle' in lower, 'Should contain a <circle> element'
assert '<rect' in lower, 'Should contain a <rect> element'
assert 'fill=' in lower, 'Shapes should have fill attributes'
hints: The <circle> element uses cx, cy, and r attributes for position and radius.
The <rect> element uses x, y, width, and height attributes.
Use fill="color" to set the shape color, e.g. fill="blue".

challenges.title: SVG Scene
challenges.description: Create an SVG element with a viewBox attribute. Inside it, include a circle, rect, line, and polygon element. Use fill and stroke attributes to style the shapes. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create an SVG scene with multiple shapes -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
lower = html.lower()
assert '<svg' in lower, 'Should contain an <svg> element'
assert 'viewbox=' in lower, 'SVG should have a viewBox attribute'
assert '<circle' in lower, 'Should contain a <circle> element'
assert '<rect' in lower, 'Should contain a <rect> element'
assert '<line' in lower, 'Should contain a <line> element'
assert '<polygon' in lower, 'Should contain a <polygon> element'
assert 'fill=' in lower, 'Shapes should have fill attributes'
assert 'stroke=' in lower, 'Shapes should have stroke attributes'
assert '</svg>' in lower, 'Should close the <svg> element'
hints: The viewBox attribute defines the coordinate system, e.g. viewBox="0 0 200 200".
The <line> element uses x1, y1, x2, y2 attributes.
The <polygon> element uses a points attribute with coordinate pairs, e.g. points="100,10 40,198 190,78".
Use stroke="color" and stroke-width="N" to add outlines.
